trait.name: Mountain Masters
entries: Red dragons prefer mountainous terrain, badlands, and any other locale where they can perch high and survey their domain.
Their preference for mountains brings them into conflict with the hilldwelling copper dragons from time to time.

trait.name: Arrogant Tyrants
entries: Red dragons fly into destructive rages and act on impulse when angered.
They are so ferocious and vengeful that they are regarded as the archetypal evil dragon by many cultures.

trait.name: Status and Slaves
entries: Red dragons are fiercely territorial and isolationist.
They make use of lesser creatures as informants, messengers, and spies.
They are most interested in news about other red dragons, with which they compete constantly for status.
When it requires servants, a red dragon demands fealty from chaotic evil humanoids.
Creatures serving a red dragon live in constant terror of being roasted and eaten for displeasing it.

trait.name: Obsessive Collectors
entries: Red dragons value wealth above all else, and their treasure hoards are legendary.
They covet anything of monetary value, and can often judge the worth of a bauble to within a copper piece at a glance.
A red dragon knows the value and provenance of every item in its hoard, along with each item's exact location.

action.name: Multiattack
entries: The red dragon makes three attacks: one with its bite and two with its claws, or four attacks with its claws.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 15 ft., one target. Hit: 40 (3d12 + 14) piercing damage, and the target must succeed on a DC 17 Constitution saving throw, or be poisoned for 1 minute. The poisoned target has resistance to all damage except poison damage.

action.name: Claws
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft., one target. Hit: 25 (4d6 + 10) slashing damage.

action.name: Tail
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +10 to hit, reach 20 ft., one target. Hit: 30 (4d6 + 14) bludgeoning damage.

action.name: Breath Weapon
entries: The red dragon exhales a cone of fire 60 feet long. Each creature in the area must make a DC 17 Dexterity saving throw, taking 50 (10d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one.

legendary.name: Molten Fury
entries: The red dragon can use its breath weapon once per day. It regains its use after 12 hours.

legendary.name: Hoarder's Curse
entries: If the dragon is reduced to 0 hit points, it can use its legendary action to summon a fire storm that deals 50 (10d6) fire damage to all creatures within 60 feet.

spellcasting.name: Divine Magic
entries: The red dragon can cast the following spells: *Fireball*, *Cone of Cold*, *Tasha's Hideous Laughter*, *Hold Person*, and *Calm Animals*.

actionTags: Bite. Melee Weapon Attack: +4 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 7 (1d10 + 2) piercing damage.
Breath Weapons (Recharge 5-6). The dragon uses one of the following breath weapons.

Fire Breath. The dragon exhales fire in a 20-foot line that is 5 feet wide. Each creature in that line must make a DC 11 Dexterity saving throw, taking 14 (4d6) fire damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.

Sleep Breath. The dragon exhales sleep gas in a 15-foot cone. Each creature in that area must succeed on a DC 11 Constitution saving throw or fall unconscious for 1 minute. This effect ends for a creature if the creature takes damage or someone uses an action to wake it.

action.name: Bite
entries: Melee Weapon Attack: +3 to hit, reach 5 ft., one target. Hit: 3 (1d4 + 1) piercing damage. If the target is a creature, it must succeed on a DC 10 Constitution saving throw or contract a disease.

The disease has the following effects: Until the disease is cured, the target can't regain hit points except by magical means, and the target's hit point maximum decreases by 3 (1d6) every 24 hours. If the target's hit point maximum drops to 0 as a result of this disease, the target dies.

entries: The thug has advantage on an attack roll against a creature if at least one of the thug's allies is within 5 feet of the creature and the ally isn't incapacitated.

entities.canonicalText: The text appears to be a stat block for a creature, but the entity name 'TRrs.Ai WARRioR' contains OCR artifacts and formatting inconsistencies. The source text lacks a clear, properly formatted creature entry with standardized headers and structure found in the Monster Manual.
